import * as globals from '/public/globals';
import Link from '/public/snippets/anchors.astro';
import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

Your Themes feature repository requires environment variables to be created in the `/environment` directory of the repository. You can store variables that apply to all environments (for example, `production`, `sandbox`, `development`) for this organization in `/environment/index.json`. If there are environment variables that are unique to an environment in this org, you must store them in a file that matches the exact name of that environment (for example, `/environment/dailyplanet-sandbox.json` would override variable set in `/environment/index.json` in the environment named `dailyplanet-sandbox`.)

:::note
You can generate the `ACCESS_TOKEN` that you need to hit the Content API in the Developer Center tile in your environment. You will need a token for prod and sandbox in your env file when switching content bases.
:::

Here is a list of the environment variables to set in your `index.json`:

<table>
    <thead>
        <tr>
            <th>Property name</th>
            <th>Data type</th>
            <th>Purpose</th>
        </tr>
    </thead>
    <tbody>
        <tr>
            <td><code>FUSION_SERVICE_WORKER</code></td>
            <td>boolean</td>
            <td>This environment variable controls if the service worker is included in the compiled bundle. The default value is <code>false</code>.</td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
            <td><code>RESIZER_TOKEN_VERSION</code></td>
            <td>string</td>
            <td>String containing the image resizer version for your organization. You should confirm your token value in the Delivery API.<br />  Example: <code>2</code></td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
            <td><code>SIGNING_SERVICE_DEFAULT_APP</code></td>
            <td>string</td>
            <td>Specifies the use of the image resizer on your blocks.<br />  Example: <code>resizer</code></td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
            <td><code>allowedImageDomains</code></td>
            <td>string</td>
            <td>Comma-separated list of non-Arc domains where images can be served from on your website.<br />  Example: <code>archive.example.com,my-non-Arc-site.com</code></td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
            <td><code>playerRoot</code></td>
            <td>url</td>
            <td>URL to root domain of POWA video player assets.<br />  Example: <code>playerRoot://d2w3jw6424abwq.cloudfront.net</code></td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
            <td><code>searchKey</code></td>
            <td>string</td>
            <td>The key specified from your site search API.</td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
            <td><code>siteStyles</code></td>
            <td>
            string object
            </td>
            <td>
            The object should contain the mapping of your site ID with the desired site ID styles as the value. For a single site, this will be a one-to-one mapping.<br />  Example (multi-site):<br />  
               
            ```
            "siteStyles": {
                "daily-intelligencer": "daily-intelligencer",
                "the-gazette": "daily-intelligencer",
                "karc": "karc"
            }
            ```

            </td>
        </tr>
        <tr>
            <td><code>videoOrg</code></td>
            <td>string</td>
            <td>Name of your organization in Video Center.</td>
        </tr>
    </tbody>
</table>
